description_long: This guard stone is a roughly hewn, irregularly shaped block of stone with a weathered, grayish surface, set on a square stone base. The stone has a natural, jagged appearance with visible striations and some discoloration, suggesting long-term exposure to the elements. One side of the stone features a noticeable indentation or cavity, likely from impact or erosion. Behind the main stone, there is a vertical post with a rectangular sign or plaque, which appears aged and partially worn, possibly indicating historical or informational content. The base is flat and slightly textured, blending with the surrounding pavement. The overall look is utilitarian and robust, typical of objects designed to prevent vehicle encroachment or protect corners in urban environments.
description: A weathered, irregular stone block set on a square base with a vertical signpost behind it, showing signs of age and exposure.
description_view_0: Front view shows the broad, flat face of the stone with the signpost directly behind it and the square base visible.
description_view_1: Side view reveals the angled, jagged profile of the stone and highlights the rough texture and discoloration.
description_view_2: Rear view displays the narrower end of the stone, the signpost, and the base, with more visible wear and a cavity.
description_view_3: Opposite side view shows the sloped surface and a prominent hole or indentation in the stone.
attribute_location: Typically placed at the edge of urban streets, near corners, driveways, or building entrances to prevent vehicle encroachment and protect infrastructure.
attribute_installation_orientation: Meant to be fully above ground, with the base flush against pavement or sidewalk surfaces and the stone upright or slightly angled.
attribute_wear_patterns: Visible chipping and smoothing on edges and corners, likely from vehicle contact and foot traffic. Discoloration and surface erosion from prolonged weather exposure, with moss or lichen growth in crevices.
attribute_historical_markings: Aged signpost with faded or worn text or symbols; no visible engravings or inscriptions on the stone itself.
